key_points: Tissues allow specialization of cells and efficient functioning in multicellular organisms
Meristematic tissues consist of actively dividing cells with dense cytoplasm and prominent nucleus
Permanent tissues are formed through differentiation and perform specialized functions
Simple permanent tissues include parenchyma, collenchyma and sclerenchyma
Complex permanent tissues include xylem and phloem which function in transport
Animal tissues are specialized for protection, support, movement and coordination
Nervous tissue helps in rapid transmission of impulses for body coordination
examples: Xylem vessels conduct water upward in plants
Phloem sieve tubes transport food to plant parts
Bone tissue supports the human skeleton
Cardiac muscles contract rhythmically in the heart
Neurons transmit electrical impulses in the nervous system
formulas: Simple permanent tissues → Parenchyma + Collenchyma + Sclerenchyma
Neuron structure → Cell body + Dendrites + Axon
exam_tips: Understand the structural differences between parenchyma, collenchyma and sclerenchyma
Remember the components of xylem and phloem
Differentiate clearly between tendon and ligament in connective tissues
Practice drawing labeled diagrams of neuron and plant tissues
Explain how tissues combine to form organs and organ systems
